_description: Known framework-specific CVEs that dependency scanners may miss. Checked by scan step 06. Refresh via Context7 when staleness > 30 days.

react.description: Unauthenticated RCE via payload decoding in Server Functions. Attacker can execute arbitrary code on the server without authentication.
DoS + source code exposure in Server Components. Server can be crashed and source code leaked via crafted requests.

supabase.description: service_role key used in client-side code. This key bypasses ALL Row Level Security policies, granting full database access to anyone who inspects the browser network tab.
supabase.fix: Move service_role usage to Edge Functions, API routes, or server-only files. Use the anon key for client-side Supabase clients.

supabase.description: Supabase .rpc() calls execute database functions. If the underlying function has no RLS policies or SECURITY DEFINER without proper checks, it bypasses row-level security.
supabase.fix: Ensure all RPC functions use SECURITY INVOKER (default) or have explicit permission checks if using SECURITY DEFINER.

nextjs.description: Server Actions are public HTTP endpoints. Any function marked 'use server' is callable by anyone with the endpoint URL, regardless of where it's imported in the UI.
nextjs.fix: Add authentication checks at the TOP of every Server Action. Never assume the caller is authenticated just because the UI is behind auth.

django.description: DoS via HashedFilesMixin. Crafted file names cause catastrophic backtracking in regex used by staticfiles storage backend.
django.detection: grep -rE '"django"' requirements.txt pyproject.toml Pipfile setup.cfg | head -5

django.description: Django DEBUG=True exposes full stack traces, settings (including SECRET_KEY), SQL queries, and template paths to any user triggering an error.
django.fix: Set DEBUG=False in production. Use environment variable: DEBUG=os.environ.get('DJANGO_DEBUG', 'False') == 'True'

django.description: Django SECRET_KEY hardcoded in settings. Used for session signing, CSRF tokens, and password reset tokens. If leaked, allows session forgery and CSRF bypass.

flask.description: Flask debug mode enables the Werkzeug debugger, which provides an interactive Python console in the browser. Any user who can trigger an exception gets arbitrary code execution on the server.
flask.fix: Never use debug=True in production. Use environment variable: app.run(debug=os.environ.get('FLASK_DEBUG', 'false').lower() == 'true')

flask.description: Flask secret_key hardcoded in source. Used for session cookie signing. If leaked, allows session forgery.
flask.detection: grep -rn 'secret_key\s*=\s*["'\''"'\''']' --include='*.py' | grep -v environ | grep -v getenv | grep -v test

fastapi.description: FastAPI CORSMiddleware with allow_origins=['*'] permits any origin to make authenticated requests, bypassing browser same-origin protections.

aiohttp.description: HTTP request smuggling via incorrect parsing of transfer-encoding headers. Allows bypassing security controls in proxy/server chains.
